results.possibleIdentity: Daniel J. Bryan was an engineer or technical professional employed by General Electric (GE) during the late 1960s and early 1970s. He was based at General Electric's facility in Fair Lawn, New Jersey, which housed the company's Components Sales Operation. His technical expertise was in the field of electrical components. Bryan was associated with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a service organization that facilitated collaborations between the technical and artistic communities. Within E.A.T., he was recorded as a technical resource available to artists requiring expertise in electrical components. Historical records from 1977 also identify a Daniel J. Bryan as a director of Care Centers, Inc. and a shareholder in the General Electric Company, suggesting a possible transition into management or executive roles within the region.
